from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic pattern.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural wording
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "{s} serves {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{s} is {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{s} is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length of {s} is {o}",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "The runway length of {s} is {o} feet",
        "runwayName": "{s} has runway {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "The length of the first runway at {s} is {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "The length of the first runway at {s} is {o} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "The first runway at {s} is numbered {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "The second runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "The length of the third runway at {s} is {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "The third runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "The length of the fourth runway at {s} is {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fourth runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "The fifth runway at {s} is numbered {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fifth runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO identifier of {s} is {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": "The location identifier of {s} is {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA identifier of {s} is {o}",
        "nativeName": "{s} is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "{s} is governed by the {o} party",
        "capital": "The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": "The language of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": "The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s} has largest city {o}",
        "mayor": "The mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "city": "{s} is a city {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "{s} jurisdiction is {o}",
        "demonym": "Residents of {s} are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "{s} operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": "{s} operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "headquarter": "The headquarters of {s} are located in {o}",
        "class": "{s} belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "{s} belongs to the division of {o}",
        "order": "{s} belongs to the order of {o}",
        "regionServed": "{s} serves the region {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "{s} uses hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "{s} flies fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": "{s} flies attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": "{s} participated in {o}",
        "countySeat": "{s} has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "{s} is led by chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "{s} has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "{s} is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to allow richer combined sentences
    from collections import defaultdict
    subj_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subj_map[t.subject].append(t)

    combined_clauses = []

    for subject, tlist in subj_map.items():
        # Helper to fetch object for a predicate (first occurrence)
        def get_obj(pred):
            for tr in tlist:
                if tr.predicate == pred:
                    return tr.object
            return None

        # Clean objects (strip quotes, format numbers, etc.)
        def clean(obj, pred=None):
            if obj is None:
                return None
            o = obj
            if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
                o = o[1:-1]
            if pred in ("language", "officialLanguage") and o.lower().endswith(" language"):
                o = o.rsplit(" ", 1)[0]
            if pred in ("division", "order", "class"):
                o = o.lower()
            # numeric handling
            try:
                has_decimal = "." in o
                num = float(o)
                if num.is_integer():
                    o = f"{int(num)}.0" if has_decimal else str(int(num))
                else:
                    o = f"{round(num, 2)}".rstrip('0').rstrip('.')
            except (ValueError, TypeError):
                pass
            return o

        # Build a complex clause for airports when relevant attributes exist
        runway_surface = clean(get_obj("1stRunwaySurfaceType"))
        runway_length = clean(get_obj("runwayLength"))
        runway_length_m = clean(get_obj("1stRunwayLengthMetre"))
        runway_length_ft = clean(get_obj("1stRunwayLengthFeet"))
        city_served = clean(get_obj("cityServed"))
        # Prefer metre length if available, otherwise generic runwayLength
        length_str = None
        if runway_length_m:
            length_str = f"{runway_length_m} metres"
        elif runway_length_ft:
            length_str = f"{runway_length_ft} feet"
        elif runway_length:
            # assume metres if numeric
            length_str = f"{runway_length} metres"
        # If we have at least surface or length, create a runway clause
        runway_clause = None
        if runway_surface and length_str:
            runway_clause = f"its first runway is made of {runway_surface} and is {length_str} long"
        elif runway_surface:
            runway_clause = f"its first runway is made of {runway_surface}"
        elif length_str:
            runway_clause = f"its first runway is {length_str} long"

        # Assemble main clause
        parts = []
        if city_served:
            parts.append(f"{subject} serves {city_served}")
        else:
            parts.append(subject)

        if runway_clause:
            parts.append(runway_clause)

        # Add any remaining predicates using generic mapping
        used_preds = {"cityServed", "1stRunwaySurfaceType", "runwayLength",
                      "1stRunwayLengthMetre", "1stRunwayLengthFeet"}
        for tr in tlist:
            if tr.predicate in used_preds:
                continue
            tmpl = phrase_map.get(tr.predicate)
            obj_clean = clean(tr.object, tr.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                clause = tmpl.format(s=tr.subject, o=obj_clean)
            else:
                clause = f"{tr.subject} has {tr.predicate} {obj_clean}"
            combined_clauses.append(clause)

        # Join the parts for this subject
        combined_clauses.append(" and ".join(parts))

    # Join all clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not combined_clauses:
        return ""
    sentence = ", ".join(combined_clauses)
    sentence = sentence.rstrip()
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
